title: Influence
subtitle: The Psychology of Persuasion — the landmark exploration of why people say yes and how the seven universal principles of influence shape decisions in business, relationships, and everyday life.
author: Robert B. Cialdini
themes: Reciprocity as the most potent compliance weapon
Commitment and consistency as self-justification drivers
Social proof as a shortcut under uncertainty
Liking as a precondition for persuasion
Authority and the power of perceived expertise
Scarcity creates desire through psychological reactance
Unity — shared identity as the seventh principle
excerpt: Human beings are wired with automatic trigger features — mental shortcuts that let us say yes without thinking. In this landmark work, Robert Cialdini reveals the seven universal principles that drive compliance: Reciprocity, Scarcity, Authority, Consistency, Liking, Social Proof, and Unity. Drawing on decades of peer-reviewed research and three years of undercover fieldwork inside sales, fundraising, and marketing organizations, Cialdini shows how these levers of influence operate — and how to defend yourself against those who use them unethically.
